name: Classic Grilled Cheese
tagline: Buttered sourdough, sharp cheddar, crisp gold crust

hero.alt: Grilled cheese with golden crust and stretchy cheddar pull
hero.caption: grilled cheese — buttered, sharp

steps.title: Butter both sides of one slice
steps.detail: Butter generously on the outside (what'll touch pan). On the inside, a thin smear too — helps the cheese cling.
steps.tip: Butter must be soft. Cold butter tears the bread.

steps.title: Layer the cheese
steps.detail: Pile cheddar evenly to the edges. Top with the second slice (also buttered outside).
steps.tip: Slice the cheese thin — thick slices won't melt before the bread burns.

steps.title: Slow to start, hot to finish
steps.detail: Cook in a dry skillet over medium-low for 4 minutes — slow start melts the cheese without scorching the bread. Then bump to medium for the last 90 seconds per side for color.
steps.tip: If the cheese isn't melted but the crust is, slap the lid on briefly.

media.caption: slow to start, hot to finish

steps.title: Rest, then cut
steps.detail: Rest 30 seconds — the cheese needs to settle so it doesn't slide. Cut on a diagonal for the longest pull.
steps.tip: A sprinkle of flaky salt on the buttered crust right after cooking is criminally good.

healthFacts: A blend of cheeses melts more evenly than a single one — try cheddar + gruyère.
Mayo on the outside crisps better than butter at the same temperature — pick your fight.
